paragraphs.body: “Family is not an important thing. It’s everything” (Joshua J. Fox). My family and I have endured in many difficult tasks while living together. We immigrated to the United States from Italy in 2011. We didn’t know much about America, but my dad wanted a better life for us, so he moved us here. Being a bilingual family in the US was not easy. The looks we got when were speaking another language out in public or when we have different social norms then the people in the US made us look like aliens. The reality is the looks were frustrating at first, but when you have someone else close to you that feels the same pain, it is not that bad.

annotations.comment: I wonder if you might tell an actual story about a moment where you experienced these "looks" to bring this narrative to life for your reader?
annotations.context_left: not easy. The looks we got when

paragraphs.body: Being a part of my family means many great things. To be disciplined, work hard, and always be respectful to others. But it also means being very loud, having a little bit of anger problems, and being a perfectionist. This is what makes my family, and we had to all work together to accomplish our goals. My dad was always a hard worker. He was the breadwinner for five kids and a wife for many years. He worked hard during the day, so his family could have fun. Although he might seem like a strict parent he really likes to joke around and have fun, as long as you were doing good in school. My mom is my superhero. She had five kids and had to take care of all of them. While doing this she was also a high school teacher in Italy and now a teacher at the University of Pittsburgh. She had to cook and clean for the whole family, even though she was tired from work or just having a bad day. I remember in 2015 when my mom was getting her doctor’s degree in education, she was working so hard that she literally had no time for free time or to just do what she wanted for once. These basics is what formed my siblings and I and what inspires us to become better. This didn’t just start with my parents. My grandparents on both sides had to work hard to help their family. On my dad’s side both my grandparents worked at a bakery that my grandfather owned, so they had to work every day to run a successful bakery in Chicago and after in Sicily. On my mom side she only had my grandmother because my grandfather passed when my mom was twelve. My grandmother worked as a doctor, while also being a single parent, and had to take care of 4 children. My family worked extremely hard every day, so everyone they loved could have a great life.

annotations.text: But it also means being very loud, having a little bit of anger problems, and being a perfectionist.
He worked hard during the day, so his family could have fun.
On my dad’s side both my grandparents worked at a bakery that my grandfather owned, so they had to work every day to run a successful bakery in Chicago and after in Sicily.

paragraphs.body: I have many languages of my own, but where I got these languages is the funny part. First my main language is that I am bilingual and can speak Italian. That is a big part of my culture and love anytime I have the opportunity to speak it. Here in the US, there is not many opportunities to speak my language, especially when I am away from my family. Then, my second most used language is soccer. Soccer is a big part of my life. My family and I all watch soccer and enjoy it very much. I consider myself a very passionate fan of soccer especially the Italian national team and Juventus. Another language I speak is my language of school. I put most of my hours in my life right now into school because I am trying to have a successful future. I am not the biggest lover of school, but it is a sacrifice that most people make to become more successful in their future. Now there is parts of school that I honestly enjoy, but I would still consider them a sacrifice because of why I would be doing it in the first place. Now the reason I said that this was funny is because most of the languages I speak have something to do with my family and how they shaped me. I only speak Italian because my parents always forced me to speak when we were in the house. I only speak my language of soccer because ever since I was born my family were lovers of soccer. My grandfather watched every single AC Milan game, and my dad watched a lot of Juventus games growing up. Also, my brothers playing soccer and going to their games to watch them play. Then finally my school language came from my parents making me go to school every day. Also, a funny thing about my family is that almost all of us are studying or studied STEM classes. It all started with my dad who studied it in college and then went with all three of my older siblings and know me too. My family had a large influence in the person I am today and in the things that I like.

annotations.text: I have many languages of my own, but where I got these languages is the funny part. First my main language is that I am bilingual and can speak Italian. That is a big part of my culture and love anytime I have the opportunity to speak it. Here in the US, there is not many opportunities to speak my language, especially when I am away from my family. Then, my second most used language is soccer. Soccer is a big part of my life.

annotations.text: Now there is parts of school that I honestly enjoy, but I would still consider them a sacrifice because of why I would be doing it in the first place.
annotations.comment: What do you mean? Why is school a sacrifice for you?

paragraphs.body: A lesson that I learned from my family and one of my favorites is that to have something good in my life I must sacrifice. I love this lesson because I think it is very important for people to understand. A sacrifice doesn’t always mean something that I have to do something that extremely inconvenient, but it could just be a task to complete to learn a lesson. My mom and dad had to sacrifice a lot to give me the life that I have now. A sacrifice that a person may have could be going to school to get a good job or working out to get fit. It could be for myself or for others. I see sacrifices as helping others. If I must sacrifice for myself, I feel like that is just a learning point in my life to learn from the past. I believe that helping others, especially the people closest to me, is an important aspect to life. Helping others can also feel good for oneself. When I help my grandmother cook and clean, I know it helps her, but it makes me feel good that I can help my grandmother and appreciate all the sacrifices that she had during her life. Right now, I would consider myself sacrificing to give my future wife and kids a better life. Sacrifies for other is an important task for my language because it helps me appreciate all the people that helped me in my life.

annotations.text: A lesson that I learned from my family and one of my favorites is that to have something good in my life I must sacrifice.
annotations.comment: Aha. Interesting. Is this notion of sacrifice also related to hard work?

paragraphs.body: “How’s your math grade?” “Can we go eat out tonight?” “Did you do good on your test?” It all comes back to the quote by Joshua J. Fox, “Family is not an important thing. It’s everything.” My family is who I grew up with and who taught me everything that I know and believe. They are who I base my social norms on and the people who help me in everyday life. My family gave me the greatest gift and that is taking care of me and giving me better opportunities. As the poem above shows, they were strict on me, especially when school was brought up, but they did this to help me improve as a person and for me to have a better future. Something I learned that my family taught me is that scarifies are a hundred percent worth the pain and sufferance for the people that you love. They showed this in many ways helping me become a better person in today’s society and also taught me life lessons that I will be using on my own kids one day. My grandfather, who passed away in 2019, one day saw me fighting with my sister and came up to me and told me that you don’t fight with family because at the end of the day the people who show up to your biggest accomplishment is your family. He said family was there when you were born, and family will be there when you die. You always treat your family in a good way because they always have your back.

annotations.text: It all comes back to the quote by Joshua J. Fox, “Family is not an important thing. It’s everything.”
annotations.comment: Nice :) I'm interested in the way you're focusing on this quote. I think it's probably at the heart of this essay.

annotations.text: He said family was there when you were born, and family will be there when you die.
annotations.comment: Fascinating. There's something very permanent and enduring about family to you. Let's talk about that idea :)
